learn.title: About Work Profiles
learn.intro: Work Profiles and Project Credits are Management 3.0 practices for making team skills visible and contributions recognized — enabling better task matching and transparent career growth.
learn.profiles_body: A Work Profile is a living document that captures a team member's skills (with proficiency levels), interests, preferred work types, and availability. It helps the team understand who knows what and match work to people better.
learn.credits_body: Project Credits are a lightweight way to recognize contributions without formal performance reviews. Team members log what they contributed and get credit points. This creates a transparent record of who does what.
levels: 2 — Novice: can handle routine tasks with guidance
3 — Competent: can work independently on standard tasks
4 — Proficient: sees the big picture, handles exceptions well
5 — Expert: deep intuition, can teach and improve the field
learn.p1: Profiles are self-maintained — people own their own data.
learn.p2: Skill matrix is team-visible but not used for evaluation.
learn.p3: Credits recognize effort, not just outcomes.
learn.p4: Leaderboards are opt-in — not every team wants public rankings.
